attributes.description: Display name of the opportunity (e.g., "Acme Corp - Q2 Renewal").
attributes.name: opportunityName
attributes.usage: Used in lists, kanban boards, and reports. Naming conventions vary widely — some orgs use "Account - Product - Year" templates; some use freeform descriptions. Anchors search and pipeline-review conversations ("the Acme renewal opp").

attributes.description: The Account / Company this opportunity is being sold to.
attributes.name: account
attributes.usage: Anchors the opportunity to its customer record. Required in most B2B models — every opportunity needs a buyer. NPSP loosens this for individual donations (which point at Household Accounts that may have auto-generated names). Drives account-rollup reporting (sum of all opportunities for one account).

attributes.description: Forecast revenue value of the opportunity.
attributes.name: opportunityAmount
attributes.usage: The headline number. Drives forecast aggregation, weighted pipeline (Amount × Probability), territory-quota reports, and individual rep compensation calculations. NPSP uses this for donation amount when Opportunity tracks donations.

attributes.description: Expected close date of the opportunity (or actual close date if already won/lost).
attributes.name: closeDate
attributes.usage: Anchors all forecast reports — "what's expected to close in Q2," "how much pipeline ends this month," "deals slipping past their close date." Required in most systems. HubSpot auto-sets this to end-of-current-month if not specified at creation.

attributes.description: Current pipeline stage of the opportunity.
attributes.name: opportunityStage
attributes.usage: Drives every pipeline visualization — kanban boards organize columns by stage; funnel reports count opportunities by stage; conversion- rate reports measure stage-to-stage progression. Stage values are heavily customized per organization. See the separate Pipeline Stage catalog entry for stage-as-an-entity treatment.

attributes.description: Likelihood the opportunity will close, expressed as a percentage.
attributes.name: probability
attributes.usage: Multiplied by Amount to produce weighted pipeline. Typically auto- derived from Stage (Prospecting = 10%, Qualification = 25%, etc.) but rep-overridable in some configurations. Salesforce auto-sets; HubSpot configures per stage in pipeline settings.

attributes.name: opportunityType
attributes.usage: Drives new-vs-expansion reporting (critical for SaaS metrics: ARR, net new ARR, expansion ARR). Also feeds renewal-rate calculations. In NPSP this becomes Donation Type (with values like Cash, Stock, In-Kind, Matching Gift) via the Type or Record Type field.

attributes.description: Channel through which this opportunity originated.
attributes.name: leadSource
attributes.usage: Carries forward from the source Lead during conversion, or set directly when the opportunity is created from a known account. Drives marketing-attribution analysis (which channels produce the most won revenue).

attributes.name: description
attributes.usage: Catch-all narrative for context that doesn't fit structured fields — deal strategy, key requirements, competitive landscape. Often duplicated by Notes attached to the opportunity.

attributes.description: Brief description of the next planned action on this opportunity.
attributes.name: nextStep
attributes.usage: Pipeline-review staple — sales managers scan this column to gauge whether reps have a credible path forward on each open deal. If Next Step is empty or stale, the deal is often flagged as at-risk.

attributes.description: The user who owns the opportunity (the responsible sales rep).
attributes.name: assignedUser
attributes.usage: Drives quota allocation, sales-rep compensation, "my pipeline" filtering, and forecast roll-up by manager. Critical for sales-org reporting; opportunity ownership is the primary attribute that determines whose number a deal counts toward.

attributes.description: The pipeline this opportunity belongs to (when multiple pipelines are configured).
attributes.name: pipeline
attributes.usage: HubSpot and Salesforce support multiple pipelines per object — for example, separate New Business and Renewals pipelines, or separate Direct Sales and Channel Sales pipelines. Each pipeline has its own stages. Required when more than one pipeline exists.

attributes.description: System-generated timestamp of when the opportunity was created.
attributes.name: createdAt
attributes.usage: Drives sales-cycle calculations (close date minus created date) and cohort reporting (deals created in Q1 — how many closed?).

business_context: Opportunity records hold pending revenue. Each opportunity represents one pursuit — one potential sale that the team is actively working to close. Opportunities have an amount (forecast revenue), a close date (when it's expected to close), a stage (where in the sales process it sits), and a probability (likelihood of closing). The combination of these fields drives every sales dashboard, every forecast, every pipeline review. Critical observation for nonprofit deployments: Salesforce NPSP repurposes the Opportunity entity to track Donations rather than sales — donation records use Opportunity as their underlying object with custom Record Types (Donation, Major Gift, Recurring Gift, Grant, In-Kind, Matching Gift, Membership). This is unusual; CiviCRM and Bloomerang treat donations as their own entity (Contribution / Transaction) rather than forcing them into a sales-pipeline shape. Attio and HubSpot use "Deal" rather than "Opportunity" but the concept is essentially the same. EspoCRM uses Opportunity. When interviewing clients, surface "Opportunity" by asking whether they need to track potential revenue through stages: yes for organizations selling products or services, also yes for nonprofits tracking grant pursuits or major gift cultivation through cultivation stages, generally no for organizations that only track donations after they happen (use Donation/Contribution/Transaction instead). Pipeline configuration matters: the stages, probabilities, and required fields per stage are typically the most-customized aspect of any Opportunity deployment.
name: Opportunity
purpose: A potential sale or deal in progress, tracked through stages from initial qualification to close. The pipeline anchor of B2B CRMs — most sales reporting (forecast, pipeline, win rate, sales cycle, average deal size) derives from Opportunity records.

relationships.description: An opportunity belongs to one account.
An opportunity has one or more associated contacts (decision-makers, influencers, champions). Modeled via OpportunityContactRole junction records in Salesforce; via Associations in HubSpot.
An opportunity has many activities (calls, meetings, tasks) logged against it.
An opportunity is owned by a user.

sources.title: CiviCRM: Contributions (donation-tracking entity, distinct from sales pipeline)
Salesforce NPSP: Organize Your Data Effectively (Opportunity with Donation Record Types)

systems.name: Deal
systems.notes: Standard object (must be enabled — not active by default in all workspaces). Designed around kanban-board pipeline visualization. Deals link to Companies and People via relationship attributes. Stages are configurable per workspace.

systems.name: Transaction (closest analog) / Pledge (for committed-but-not-paid)
systems.notes: MAJOR DIVERGENCE. Bloomerang does not have a sales-pipeline Opportunity entity. Donations are recorded as Transactions (already-paid) or Pledges (committed, not yet paid). Major-gift cultivation pursuits aren't first-class entities in Bloomerang — typically tracked via Custom Fields on the Constituent or via Interactions logging cultivation steps.

systems.name: Contribution (closest analog) / Case (for cultivation)
systems.notes: MAJOR DIVERGENCE. CiviCRM does not have a sales-pipeline Opportunity entity. Donations are tracked via Contribution records (which are already-completed, not pending). Cultivation pursuits are sometimes modeled via Case records, which have stages and assignments, but Case is purpose-built for support tickets rather than sales pipeline. Sales-style pipeline would require custom entity development.

systems.name: Opportunity
systems.notes: Standard CRM-module entity. Includes name, amount, closeDate, stage (enum), probability (auto-derived from stage by default), leadSource, assignedUser. Stage enum ships with values like Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/ Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost.

systems.name: Deal
systems.notes: HubSpot uses "Deal" rather than "Opportunity" but the concept is identical. Standard properties: dealname, amount, closedate, dealstage, pipeline, dealtype, hubspot_owner_id. Deals support multiple pipelines (each with its own stages). dealstage values are configurable per pipeline.

systems.name: Opportunity
systems.notes: Foundational standard object. Opportunity has standard fields like Amount, CloseDate, StageName (picklist), Probability (numeric, often auto-derived from Stage), Type (New Business / Existing Business), LeadSource. Each opportunity belongs to one Account. Opportunity Products (line items) attach via OpportunityLineItem.

systems.name: Opportunity (with Donation Record Types)
systems.notes: Same Salesforce Opportunity object, but NPSP overlays multiple Record Types: Donation, Major Gift, Grant, In-Kind Gift, Matching Gift, Membership, Recurring Donation. Each Record Type has different page layouts and field visibility. NPSP uses Closed Won = donation received; Closed Lost = donation declined / lapsed. The entity itself is universal Opportunity; the subclass happens via Record Types.
